0.breadcrumbs: Operating System » Before the Beginning » Before the Beginning
0.title: Before the Beginning

1.breadcrumbs: Operating System » Before the Beginning » Live USB
1.title: Live USB

10.breadcrumbs: Operating System » First Steps » System Clock
10.title: System Clock

11.breadcrumbs: Operating System » First Steps » Partition the Disks
11.title: Partition the Disks

12.body: To identify available disks, both lsblk and fdisk -l can be used.
12.breadcrumbs: Operating System » First Steps » Device Identification
12.title: Device Identification

13.body: To write the partition tables using cfdisk: cfdisk /dev/the_disk_to_be_partitioned Note: make sure to set the correct type for the efi partition. Example Layout: Mount point Purpose Suggested size Partition type /efi efi 1 GiB EFI System [SWAP] swap 8 GiB Linux filesystem / root Remainder Linux filesystem To verify the partitions: fdisk -l /dev/partitioned_disk
13.breadcrumbs: Operating System » First Steps » Partition Tables
13.title: Partition Tables

14.body: To create an Ext4 file system for the root: mkfs.ext4 /dev/root_partition To initialize the swap partition: mkswap /dev/swap_partition To format the EFI partition: mkfs.fat -F 32 /dev/efi_system_partition
14.breadcrumbs: Operating System » First Steps » Formatting Partitions
14.title: Formatting Partitions

15.body: Mount the root volume to /mnt: mount /dev/root_partition /mnt Mount the EFI partition: mount --mkdir /dev/efi_system_partition /mnt/efi Enable the swap volume: swapon /dev/swap_partition
15.breadcrumbs: Operating System » First Steps » Mounting the File Systems
15.title: Mounting the File Systems

17.body: Update the mirrorlist using reflector: reflector --protocol https --verbose --latest 25 --sort rate --save /etc/pacman.d/mirrorlist
17.title: Mirror List

18.body: pacstrap -K /mnt base linux linux-firmware base-devel nano networkmanager
18.breadcrumbs: Operating System » Installation » Install Essential Packages
18.title: Install Essential Packages

2.body: Download the ISO file from https://archlinux.org/download/ . Note: the BitTorrent Download is the recommended option and the file will look like this archlinux-version-x86_64.iso.torrent.
2.breadcrumbs: Operating System » Before the Beginning » Download the ISO
2.title: Download the ISO

21.body: Set the time zone: ln -sf /usr/share/zoneinfo/Region/City /etc/localtime Note: You can verify it running date. Generate the /etc/adjtime file: hwclock --systohc
21.title: Time

22.body: Uncomment the #en_US.UTF-8 UTF-8 line editing the /etc/locale.gen file: nano /etc/locale.gen Generate the locales: locale-gen Create the locale.conf file with the following content: nano /etc/locale.conf LANG=en_US.UTF-8
22.title: Localization

23.body: Create the hostname with your host name, e.g. desktop: nano /etc/hostname desktop
23.title: Hostname

25.body: To add a user with sudo privileges: useradd -m -G wheel <username> To add a password to the user: passwd <username> Edit the sudoers uncommenting the # %wheel ALL=(ALL:ALL) ALL line: EDITOR=nano visudo
25.breadcrumbs: Operating System » Installation » Adding a User
25.title: Adding a User

26.body: To enable NetworkManager service: systemctl enable NetworkManager
26.title: Enable Network

27.body:
Install systemd-boot bootctl install Edit the /efi/loader/loader.conf file: nano /efi/loader/loader.conf default arch.conf
timeout 4
console-mode auto Create the /efi/loader/entries/arch.conf file: nano /efi/loader/entries/arch.conf title Arch Linux
linux /vmlinuz-linux
initrd /initramfs-linux.img
options root=UUID=xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx rw To make it easy to set the UUID you can use the blkid command and append the output to the arch.conf file. blkid -s UUID /dev/root_partition >> /efi/loader/entries/arch.conf Copy everything that is inside the /boot path to /efi.
27.title: Boot Loader

28.body: Exit the chroot environment: exit Unmount all the partitions and reboot: umount -R /mnt reboot That's it. Good luck!
28.title: Reboot

29.body: If you have succeeded, there's one last thing to be done. First download fastfetch: sudo pacman -S fastfetch Now simply run and enjoy your achievement: fastfetch
29.breadcrumbs: Operating System » Installation » By the Way
29.title: By the Way

3.body: Find out the name of your USB drive: ls -l /dev/disk/by-id/usb-* Make sure it is not mounted: lsblk
3.breadcrumbs: Operating System » Before the Beginning » Find the USB drive name
3.title: Find the USB drive name

31.body: Refresh the entire system: sudo pacman -Syyu Make sure to have some basic system packages installed: sudo pacman -S --needed base-devel bash curl gcc git ncurses xz zstd
31.title: System Check

32.body: Install clang: sudo pacman -S clang Install go: sudo pacman -S go Install rustup: sudo pacman -S rustup rustup update stable
32.title: Compilers

33.body:
Install amd-ucode or intel-ucode according with your CPU. sudo pacman -S amd-ucode sudo pacman -S intel-ucode Install booster: sudo pacman -S booster Copy both microcode and booster images to the /efi path. Create new loader entry including both new initrd images accordingly: sudo nano /efi/loader/entries/booster.conf title Arch Linux with Booster
linux /vmlinuz-linux
initrd /cpu_manufacturer-ucode.img
initrd /booster-linux.img
options root=UUID=xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx rw You can take the opportunity to include other kernel parameters to the option line. For example nvme_load=YES, nowatchdog, quiet, splash, etc. To check the parameters your system was booted up with: cat /proc/cmdline Update the /efi/loader/loader.conf file: sudo nano /efi/loader/loader.conf To use the booster.conf as default: default booster.conf
timeout 4
console-mode auto To verify the boot loader entries: sudo bootctl list
33.title: New Initrd

34.body: Update pacman configuration file: sudo nano /etc/pacman.conf An example can be found on EndeavourOS repo
34.title: Pacman

35.body: Create and cd into an apps directory: mkdir apps && cd apps Install yay: git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/yay.git && cd yay && makepkg -si First use: yay -Y --gendb
35.title: Yay

36.breadcrumbs: Operating System » Drivers and Modules » Drivers and Modules
36.title: Drivers and Modules

37.breadcrumbs: Operating System » Drivers and Modules » Graphics
37.title: Graphics

38.body: For Vulkan support: sudo pacman -S vulkan-radeon
38.breadcrumbs: Operating System » Drivers and Modules » AMD
38.title: AMD

39.body: To early load modules using Booster: sudo nano /etc/booster.yaml modules_force_load: <module_name>
39.breadcrumbs: Operating System » Drivers and Modules » Kernel Mode Setting
39.title: Kernel Mode Setting

4.body: Write the ISO to the USB drive using pv: sudo pv path/to/archlinux-version-x86_64.iso -Yo /dev/disk/by-id/usb-My_flash_drive-0:0 Note: the USB drive name should look something like /dev/disk/by-id/usb-SanDisk_Cruzer_Blade_4C530000100920118104-0:0. Make sure to not append any partition number like My_flash_drive-0:0-part1. For more information: https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/USB_flash_installation_medium
4.breadcrumbs: Operating System » Before the Beginning » Write the ISO
4.title: Write the ISO

41.breadcrumbs: Desktop Environment » Home Sweet Home » Home Sweet Home
41.title: Home Sweet Home

42.body: Install chezmoi: sudo pacman -S chezmoi Initialize it: chezmoi init https://github.com/<user>/dotfiles.git Apply it: chezmoi apply Note: a lot of packages will be installed. Probably would be a good idea to reboot the system after it?
42.breadcrumbs: Desktop Environment » Home Sweet Home » Bootstrap
42.title: Bootstrap

43.body:
To enable the greetd service: systemctl enable greetd Edit greetd configuration file to use tuigreet: sudo nano /etc/greetd/config.toml [terminal]
vt = 1 [default_session]
command = "tuigreet --remember --remember-session --asterisks"
user = "greeter"
43.breadcrumbs: Desktop Environment » Home Sweet Home » Greeter
43.title: Greeter

44.body: Launching niri from the tty: niri-session
44.breadcrumbs: Desktop Environment » Home Sweet Home » Graphical Interface
44.title: Graphical Interface

45.body: Check if Nushell is in the valid shells list: cat /etc/shells To set Nushell as login shell: chsh -s /usr/bin/nu <user> Reboot the system to check if everything is ok.
45.breadcrumbs: Desktop Environment » Home Sweet Home » Shell
45.title: Shell

46.breadcrumbs: Desktop Environment » Quality of Life » Quality of Life
46.title: Quality of Life

47.body: To refresh the font cache: sudo fc-cache -fv
47.breadcrumbs: Desktop Environment » Quality of Life » Font
47.title: Font

48.body: To generate a new SSH key: ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "your_email@example.com" Check the public key: cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub Test the SSH connection: ssh -T git@github.com
48.breadcrumbs: Desktop Environment » Quality of Life » SSH
48.title: SSH

49.body:
To install and set Neovim nightly using bob: bob install nightly sudo bob use nightly To increase the maximum number of inotify watches and queued events. Add the following to /etc/sysctl.conf sysctl fs.inotify.max_user_watches=100000
sysctl fs.inotify.max_queued_events=100000
49.breadcrumbs: Desktop Environment » Quality of Life » Neovim
49.title: Neovim

5.breadcrumbs: Operating System » First Steps » First Steps
5.title: First Steps

6.body: Change the font using setfont: setfont ter-222b
6.breadcrumbs: Operating System » First Steps » Quality of Life
6.title: Quality of Life

7.breadcrumbs: Operating System » First Steps » Some verification
7.title: Some verification

8.body: The following command should return 64: cat /sys/firmware/efi/fw_platform_size
8.breadcrumbs: Operating System » First Steps » UEFI Mode
8.title: UEFI Mode
